name: Scottish Scones
recipeIngredient: 1 Egg, at room temperature, lightly beaten
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, mix together the flour, salt, baking soda, and cream of tartar. Stir thoroughly.
2. With your fingers, rub the butter into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Gradually stir in the beaten egg and buttermilk. If the dough is too moist and sticky, add a bit more flour.
4. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead as little as possible to form a soft, pliable dough ball.
5. Divide the dough into two equal parts. Flatten each portion with your fingers into a round disc about 6 inches in diameter and 1/2 inch thick.
6. Prick each flattened piece about a dozen times with a fork.
7. Cut each flat piece into four sections.
8. Bake on a lightly greased baking sheet at 375°F (190°C) for about 15 minutes, or until golden brown. You can add 1/2 cup raisins or currants if desired.
